question: What's ISEN 625 about?
reference_answer:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

• Understand the fundamental methodologies of discrete event (process-
oriented) simulation modeling.
• Understand the key statistical issues involved in simulation data preparation
and the analysis of simulation output.
• Become familiar with modeling using both a general-purpose language
(Python) and a commercial language (Simio).

question: What's ISEN 350 about?
reference_answer:
UWRT - University Required Writing Intensive

ISEN 350 is now certified as a Writing (W) course. In ISEN 350, you'll put Quality
Engineering methods into practice through hands-on lab activities and document your
findings in professional technical reports. Because this is a Writing (W) course, you'll also
develop strong written communication skills that are highly valued by employers. Faculty
from the ISEN Writing and Student Success Center will be available to guide and support
you in becoming a more confident and effective technical communicator.

question: What's ISEN 632 about?
reference_answer:
Human Factors Engineering for Aerospace Designs. (3-0). Credit 3. Physiological
and psychophysiological issues encountered in aviation and space environments
and their effect on human cognitive and physical performance; survey methods for
human workload and performance evaluations; apply human-systems design
principles to an aerospace design project. Prerequisites: Graduate classification or
approval of instructor. Cross-listed with AERO 636.

question: Given the flipped classroom format of ISEN 350, how much time outside of the Wednesday meetings should I expect to dedicate to the lecture videos, readings, and problem sets each week to succeed in the course?
reference_answer:
ISEN 350 will follow a flipped classroom format. Classes will meet on Wednesdays, and next week's module will be posted at 1 PM. Weekly modules begin with an overview and include lecture videos, a reading assignment, a problem set, and a module quiz. Most lecture videos are brief—approximately 20 minutes—and are designed to introduce key concepts. While they are not comprehensive, they will serve as a foundation for each module and will guide your study. Further amplification of the material will be found in the textbook, reference materials, and classroom discussions.

Success in this course requires active engagement. You are fully responsible for your own learning and performance. I am he

question: Besides this course, what other TAMU courses would cover topics like reinforcement learning and dynamic programming in depth?
reference_answer:
Topic
1 Review of Markov Processes, Ergodic Theory and Stationary Processes.
2 Markov Decision Process (MDP), Examples: Inventory Control, Linear Quadratic Control.

10 Policy Gradients.

question: For ISEN 637, what's considered equivalent to ISEN 609 for the prerequisites?
Has the grading scheme for ISEN 302 been modified since the syllabus was released?
reference_answer: The grading scheme may be modified at the discretion of the instructor if a situation arises where it may be necessary and any such changes would be announced at least a week in advance.

question: In ISEN 302, does "engineering design" in the learning outcomes specifically include consideration of environmental factors?

question: Did ISEN 310 adequately prepare you to understand and use random variables and expectations to solve engineering problems?
reference_answer:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
• Explain basic concepts behind sample spaces, probability laws, random variables, and independence.
• Apply mathematical descriptions of random variables and the notion of expectation in solving engineering problems.
• Analyze data by means of computing point estimates and confidence intervals; and estimating/fitting distributions.

question: Do the ISEN 460 prerequisites seem necessary for success in the course?
reference_answer: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in ISEN 210, ISEN 330, ISEN 340, ISEN 350, ISEN 355 and ISEN 370; junior or senior classification.
